## Type of Change
<!-- Mark relevant options with an 'x' -->

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation update
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (no functional changes, no API changes)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement
- [ ] ✅ Test addition/improvement
- [ ] 🔧 Configuration/build changes

## Code Quality Checklist

### Standards
- [ ] My code follows the project's coding standards (PEP 8, project conventions)
- [ ] I have run `ruff check streamrip` (no errors)
- [ ] I have run `ruff format streamrip` (code formatted)
- [ ] I have added/updated type hints where appropriate
- [ ] I have added/updated docstrings for new functions/classes

### Testing
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally (`poetry run pytest`)
- [ ] I have tested edge cases and error conditions

### Documentation
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation (README, wiki, docstrings)
- [ ] I have updated CODEBASE_REVIEW.md if resolving action items
- [ ] I have added comments for complex logic
- [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md (if applicable)

### Security
- [ ] I have checked my code for security vulnerabilities
- [ ] I have not introduced any hardcoded secrets or credentials
- [ ] I have validated all user inputs
- [ ] I have reviewed SECURITY.md for relevant guidelines

- [ ] Breaking changes are well-documented
